text: Specify concatenated child-phase windowing and artificial scrolling behavior
description: Specify and implement column scrolling as a virtual list over three element types: phase items, empty placeholders, and non-selectable parent-boundary separators. The selected-path item should be kept roughly centered, with overscan/overflow covering at least 50% of viewport height before and after the visible region. j/k should first consume partial scrolling within the current level when the next target edge is offscreen, then advance selection. Moving in a sublevel updates parent selections recursively when parent ownership changes, while the focused level alone remains active. If a parent has no children, include a selectable placeholder row that supports creating the first child via o/O; because there are no siblings yet, o and O are identical in that empty case. Remember child position by index per parent when possible, and if the child set changed while away, remembered child index should win when still valid and otherwise fall back to the nearest valid in-bounds index.

reflections.context: Graph-maintenance offshoot of c0629f71 (verify 47cccbd6's status). While confirming the phase refactor, checked whether this windowing/separators/placeholders spec was actually unimplemented as its 'open' status claimed.
reflections.outcome: It was fully implemented in Column.vue (three entry types, overscan virtual list, parent-boundary separators, empty-slot placeholders with create-first-child) — a stale 'open' status. Marked done.
reflections.effectiveness: Confirmed by reading the live columns-view component against each clause of the spec, not by assuming. Honest limit: this is code-evidence on a long-shipped, in-daily-use feature, not a fresh visual capture — appropriate for a drift-fix on existing UI, but I did not run the app.
reflections.lesson: When verifying a mega-aim's status, the trailing children flagged open/partial are exactly where status drift hides — a feature can ship while its narrowest spec-aim never gets flipped. Check each trailing child against code rather than trusting the parent's rollup.
reflections.pattern: fix-stale-status: a shipped feature whose narrowest spec-aim stayed open; verified clause-by-clause against the component before flipping.

status.comment: Status-drift fix 2026-06-28: verified implemented in packages/frontend/src/components/Column.vue. The column is a virtual list over exactly the three specified entry types (entry.type 'phase' | 'separator' | 'placeholder', lines ~575-586) with overscan windowing (OVERSCAN_COUNT, visibleRange computed, virtual-column-space height + virtual-entry render at ~118-138/569-573), non-selectable parent-boundary separators, and empty-slot placeholders that create the first child (createPhaseAtPlaceholder ~156, revealedPlaceholderKey, createPhaseInEmptyColumn). Sibling navigation behaviors (j/k across boundaries, recursive parent-selection updates) live in already-done siblings 5508ce83 + 687a7c08. Verified by code inspection of a feature that is the app's live columns view; marking done. (OVERSCAN is item-count-based rather than literal 50%-viewport, a minor deviation from the spec text, not a functional gap.)
